title: Lower Tethers
desc: The lower chamber's anchor lines are drifting out of true, which makes an already strange workspace less forgiving.

steps.text:
Below the main chamber, the lower reaches of Crystal Cave behave differently from the world above them. Weight is not absent there, but it grows uncertain enough that loose tools wander, careless steps travel too far, and every work lane is strung with anchor lines for creatures sensible enough to use them. It is one of those places where local custom and local physics arrived at the same conclusion: tie yourself to something.

The lower keeper hands you a coil of fresh line and a pouch of weighted clips. "Inspect the anchor run if you want the longer circuit," she says. "Or reset the tool tether points on the work shelf if you prefer smaller fixes in a stranger room. Either way, do not trust the chamber just because it looks calm."

options.label: Inspect the full anchor run through the lower chamber
Reset the weighted tool tethers on the work shelf

steps.text:
The main anchor run crosses the lower chamber in a set of measured spans between iron eyes driven into crystal-free pockets of stone. Three segments are still sound. The fourth has slackened enough that a bad misstep would send a worker drifting farther than the route allows before the line caught. You replace the worn section, draw the new length tight, and test each span with the keeper leaning back in full harness until the run answers with a firm, reassuring pull instead of a languid curve.

The chamber never stops being strange. Dust hangs a little longer than it should. Your own steps feel argumentative. But once the anchor line is true again, the strangeness returns to being manageable instead of mischievous. The keeper clips in, walks the line once, and nods. "Good. Mystery is acceptable. Slack is not."

options.label: Coil the old line and leave the anchor run true

steps.text:
The work shelf along the lower wall uses weighted tool tethers so chisels, lamps, and sample bags do not decide to wander off into whatever the chamber considers down today. Two weights have cracked and another clip has bent enough that the keeper no longer trusts it near sharp crystal. You replace the bad weights, fit fresh clips, and test each tether with a deliberate push until the tools drift back to hand instead of out into the open chamber.

It is a small satisfaction, watching the stubborn little system restore order to a room that has unusual opinions about order in the first place. When the last tether settles properly, the shelf stops looking like a cautionary tale and starts looking like a workstation again. The keeper gathers the repaired tools and says, "There. Strange conditions are tolerable. Lost equipment is merely embarrassing."

options.label: Hang the tools back in place and clear the shelf
